old_claim.note: Town Market Squares provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Trade Routes provides the exchange and transport context that made standardized storage amphorae useful for moving oil, wine, grain, fish, and other goods beyond a single household or workshop.

source_shape.source_locator: The overview describes amphorae as containers delivered by land or sea and says stamps, inscriptions, and contents help historians reconstruct ancient trade networks.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Storage amphorae are trade and transport containers, not primarily market-square institutions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source directly ties amphora form, contents, inscriptions, and land/sea delivery to trade-network reconstruction. Town market squares are downstream exchange venues and are broader than the container technology.

ontology_before: Storage Amphorae depended on Town Market Squares, which made a public market venue appear to enable ceramic storage-container form.
ontology_after: Storage Amphorae depends on Trade Routes for the movement and exchange context while keeping Pottery Wheel and Food Preservation as production/content context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pottery_storage_amphorae now depends on trade_routes and no longer directly depends on town_market_squares.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to amphorae used only as retail stall containers.
A source shows the earliest standardized storage amphorae required town market squares rather than broader trade routes.
A separate market_amphora_retail node is added and needs town_market_squares.
short_reason: Storage amphorae are transport and storage containers, not market-square infrastructure.
